latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
31 Mar 2026 14:02
RNS Number : 8953Y
Tekmar Group PLC
31 March 2026
31 March 2026
Tekmar Group plc
("Tekmar", the "Company" or the "Group")
Result of AGM
Tekmar Group plc (AIM: TGP), a leading provider of
asset protection technology and offshore energy services
, announces that at the Annual General Meeting ("AGM") held at 1pm today, all resolutions put before shareholders were duly passed. A summary of votes cast for each resolution relating to the AGM will shortly be made available on the Group's website at:
https://investors.tekmar.co.uk/investors/investor-centre/
Enquiries:
Tekmar Group plc
Richard Turner, CEO
Phil Lanigan, CFO
c/o +44 (0)20 4582 3500
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Peter Lynch
Neil McDonald
Pearl Kellie
+44 (0)131 220 9772
+44 (0)131 220 9771
+44 (0)131 220 9775
Gracechurch Group (Financial Media & Investor Relations)
Murdo Montgomery
Alexis Gore
+44 (0)20 4582 3500
About Tekmar Group plc
Tekmar Group plc collaborates with its partners to deliver robust and sustainable engineering led solutions that enable the world's energy transition.
Through our Offshore Energy and Marine Civils Divisions we provide a range of engineering services and technologies to support and protect offshore wind farms and other offshore energy assets and marine infrastructure. With near 40 years of experience, we optimise and de-risk projects, solve customer's engineering challenges, improve safety and lower project costs. Our capabilities include geotechnical design and analysis, simulation and engineering analysis, bespoke equipment design and build, subsea protection technology and subsea stability technology.
We have a clear strategy focused on strengthening Tekmar's value proposition as an engineering solutions-led business which offers integrated and differentiated technology, services and products to our global customer base.
Headquartered in Newton Aycliffe, UK, Tekmar Group has an extensive global reach with offices, manufacturing facilities, strategic supply partnerships and representation in 18 locations across Europe, Africa, the Middle East, Asia Pacific and North America.
For more information visit:
www.tekmargroup.com
Subscribe to further news from Tekmar Group at
Group News
.

latest.title: £2m Contract Awards on Japanese Offshore Project
latest.content:
31 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7113Y
Tekmar Group PLC
31 March 2026
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR. Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is considered to be in the public domain.
Tekmar Group plc
("Tekmar", the "Company" or the "Group")
£2m of Contract Awards on Japanese Offshore Wind Project
Tekmar Group plc (AIM: TGP), a leading provider of asset protection technology and offshore energy services, is pleased to announce the award of two contracts, with a combined value of £2 million, for an offshore wind farm project in Japan.
The main contract involves the supply of Tekmar's market leading 10th generation Cable Protection Technology directly to the wind farm developer. Work is scheduled to commence immediately, with revenue expected to be recognised across the remainder of FY26 and into H1 FY27.
The award follows on from Tekmar providing the preliminary technical analysis, and design work on this project, reflecting the strength of its relationship with this customer and the value of Tekmar's integrated in-house engineering capabilities.
This builds on Tekmar's established presence in the Asia-Pacific region and supports the Group's continued success in the increasingly globally diversified offshore wind market, and its long-term growth ambitions set out in Project Aurora.
Richard Turner, CEO of Tekmar Group plc, said:
"This award highlights the value of our integrated engineering and technology offering, and our ability to partner with customers throughout the full project lifecycle. It further demonstrates our ongoing success in building a sustainable and diversified order book with multi-year visibility."
Enquiries:
Tekmar Group plc
Richard Turner, CEO
Phil Lanigan, CFO
c/o +44 (0)20 4582 3500
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Peter Lynch
Neil McDonald
Pearl Kellie
+44 (0)131 220 9772
+44 (0)131 220 9771
+44 (0)131 220 9775
Gracechurch Group (Financial Media & Investor Relations)
Murdo Montgomery
Alexis Gore
+44 (0)20 4582 3500
About Tekmar Group plc
Tekmar Group plc is a leading provider of asset protection technology and offshore energy services, supporting the global energy transition and marine infrastructure markets.
Operating across two core divisions - Asset Protection Technology and Offshore Energy Services - the Group delivers proprietary, engineering-led solutions that protect, stabilise and extend the life of critical offshore assets, including offshore wind farms, subsea interconnectors, oil and gas infrastructure, and ports and harbours.
With 40 years of experience, Tekmar has delivered asset protection and stabilisation solutions to more than 120 offshore wind projects across 25 countries, supporting over 40GW of installed capacity globally. The Group has deployed over 12,000 cable protection systems, supplied more than 100,000 subsea stabilisation products, and delivered in excess of 300,000 geotechnical and analysis hours, underpinned by a team of specialist engineers across multiple disciplines.
Through continuous innovation and scalable engineering and manufacturing capability, Tekmar supports increasingly complex offshore energy and marine infrastructure developments worldwide.
Headquartered in Newton Aycliffe, UK, Tekmar Group operates internationally with offices, manufacturing facilities, strategic supply partnerships and representation across Europe, Africa, the Middle East, Asia- Pacific and North America.
For more information visit:
www.tekmargroup.com
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Notice of AGM & Publication of Annual Report
latest.content:
9 Mar 2026 07:17
RNS Number : 8414V
Tekmar Group PLC
09 March 2026
Tekmar Group plc
("Tekmar", the "Company" or the "Group")
Notice of AGM and Publication of Annual Report
Tekmar Group plc (AIM: TGP), a leading provider of asset protection technology and offshore energy services, announces that its Annual General Meeting ("AGM") will be held at 1pm on 31 March 2026 at the offices of Muckle LLP, Time Central, 32 Gallowgate, Newcastle upon Tyne, NE1 4BF.
The Notice of Annual General Meeting, along with the Company's annual report and accounts for the year ended 30 September 2025, have been published and are available on the Company's website at:
https://investors.tekmar.co.uk/investors/investor-centre/
and are being posted to shareholders who have elected to receive physical copies.

company_30s.what_they_do: TGP Ltd. builds and supplies specialised offshore wind turbines, marine logistics platforms and port‑development infrastructure. They design, fabricate and install the heavy‑lift equipment that powers wind farms and moves cargo in marine environments.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The offshore wind sector is expanding at 10‑15% a year in Europe and the Middle East. TGP’s contracts for large wind farms and port projects give it a foothold in a market that needs reliable, high‑strength solutions. Its technology reduces installation times and costs, giving it a competitive edge in a capital‑intensive industry.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a growth‑phase, securing new contracts and raising equity to fund projects. It is still cash‑burning but has a pipeline that should generate revenue in 2026‑27. The share sits well below what the contracts imply, so the market is ignoring the upside.

rns_narrative: TGP has announced a series of multi‑million‑pound contracts: a €4m offshore wind farm win, a logistics port development award, and a Middle‑East contract. These wins add a predictable revenue stream that will start in 2026.

Alongside the contracts, the company has been raising capital – issuing equity, changing the CFO and board, and filing for AIM Rule 17 updates – signalling a focus on strengthening balance‑sheet resilience.
The narrative shows a company that is pivoting from a cash‑burning start‑up to a contract‑centric revenue generator, with the contracts acting as a stabilising catalyst.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around contract wins – the chatter is sparse but the tone is cautiously hopeful.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the large wind‑farm deal and the potential for a surge when the market recognises the company’s value. They expect the stock to rise once the first contract is executed.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue that the company still has significant capital needs, the contracts are long‑term and may not materialise quickly, and that the share price has been depressed by a lack of liquidity.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from indifferent to mildly bullish after the recent contract announcements, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – there is no public interest signal. A sudden news event could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Official signing of the €4m offshore wind contract (likely Q2 2026).
Release of Q1 2026 financials showing the first revenue from the port development project.
Any equity tranche that improves cash reserves, reducing the risk of a liquidity crunch.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike of 30‑50% above average and a price break above the 20‑day moving average. A clear upward trend in the 52‑week low area would confirm a mean‑reversion start.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a recovery. A breakout from the 52‑week low would be a key confirmation. Until then, the market is still in a capitulation phase.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share rebounds to the middle of its 52‑week range (roughly 60% of the peak), the upside is around 200% from the current price. The deep crash combined with a strong contract pipeline creates a classic mean‑reversion scenario. The signal colour would be GREEN (deep crash) if the system had flagged it, giving a 40% win rate historically.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still needs to raise capital and the contracts are long‑term. If the equity issue dilutes shareholders or the contracts fall through, the price could stay near its low. A 30% downside from the current level is realistic if the market remains unconvinced.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30% of the entry price. Potential gain: 200% if the stock recovers to mid‑52‑week levels. Risk‑reward ratio: roughly 1:6.6 – a highly asymmetric trade that favours the long side.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the recent 20‑day high with at least 20% volume lift. Size 5–10% of the portfolio, set a stop 5% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a contract signing or earnings release triggers a volume surge. If the price crosses the 20‑day moving average with 30% higher volume, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, delisting, or a material fraud claim, exit immediately. No other terminal risk is currently evident.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TGP sits at a deep crash level with a strong contract pipeline and zero public interest – a classic contrarian setup that could deliver a 200% upside if the market finally recognises the value.
